commit.message:
Add requirements.txt and update boto3 minimum to 1.42.0

TTL support in cachePoint requires boto3 >= 1.42.0 (older versions
lack the ttl field in CachePointBlock). Consolidated dependency into
a single requirements.txt and updated all README/notebook references.

Fix Sonnet 4.6 token threshold (2,048 not 1,024) and expand system prompts

Corrected minimum cache checkpoint threshold for Claude Sonnet 4.6
from 1,024 to 2,048 tokens across all scripts, notebooks, and docs.
Expanded system prompt content to exceed threshold. Fixed tenant
isolation notebook (cannot combine temperature + topP). All demos
validated locally.

Rename test_ scripts to demo_ to avoid pytest/unittest discovery confusion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix prompt-caching docs: model versions, cache key names, Nova restructure

- Add Opus 4.6/Sonnet 4.6 to supported models table
- Fix troubleshooting cache metric key names (cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens)
- Add Nova 2 Lite to token thresholds table
- Move Nova notebook from top-level nova/ into invoke_model_api/nova/, remove redundant Converse API cells
- Add TTL ordering constraint to Converse vs InvokeModel comparison table
- Document required anthropic_version field in Anthropic InvokeModel README

Nova InvokeModel notebook updates (streaming, cache metric extraction, inline content) to follow in a subsequent commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rewrite prompt-caching/ with API-type structure, streaming examples, and test scripts

Replace 4 flat notebooks with a structured layout organized by API type:

- converse_api/: 6 notebooks and 4 test scripts covering Converse + ConverseStream
  (message content, system prompts, tools, mixed TTL, tenant isolation, LangChain)
- invoke_model_api/anthropic/: 3 notebooks and 4 test scripts covering InvokeModel +
  InvokeModelWithResponseStream (message content, system prompts, tools, mixed TTL)
- nova/: existing Nova notebook moved into subfolder with updated README
- READMEs at each level with model support status, token thresholds, API comparison,
  and script usage guides

commit.message: fix FinQA currency extraction, add GPT-OSS-20B to README

Adding GPT OSS  and Qwen RFT with OpenAI compatible APIs (#681)

* Add GPT-OSS 20B reinforcement fine-tuning example with GSM8K dataset

Adds a notebook demonstrating RFT on the GPT-OSS 20B model via Bedrock's
OpenAI-compatible APIs, including file upload, job creation, monitoring,
checkpoints, inference, and base vs fine-tuned model benchmarking. Includes
the GSM8K reward function and training/validation data.

* Fix helpers import and update notebook for GPT-OSS RFT example

- Fix invalid Python import syntax (from ../../helpers) by using
  sys.path to add project root
- Add missing imports (sys, os, json, time) needed by downstream cells
- Update reward function reference to gsm8k_gptoss_rew_func.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Qwen RFT example with GSM8K dataset

- Add Qwen GSM8K reinforcement fine-tuning notebook
- Add Qwen training and validation data files
- Minor notebook execution count update in GPT-OSS example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address reviewer comments on both notebooks

- Remove redundant AWS_REGION assignment; derive MANTLE_ENDPOINT
  and provide_token region from the single definition in config cell
- Remove conflicting inline comment on reinforcement type
- Add hyperparameter descriptions to create job markdown cell

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove internal references from reward function docstring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move shared training data to datasets/ and fix Qwen notebook references

- Move rft_train_data.jsonl and rft_test_validation_data.jsonl from
  model-specific dirs to shared datasets/ directory
- Update TRAINING_FILE_PATH in both notebooks to point to ../../datasets/
- Fix GPT-OSS references in Qwen notebook (summary, benchmarking intro,
  reward function prose) to reference Qwen3 32B instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove unused variables and fix hardcoded regions

- Remove unused S3_TRAINING_DATA, S3_VALIDATION_DATA, S3_OUTPUT_PATH,
  HF_DATASET, LOCAL_DATA_DIR, and s3_client
- Use AWS_REGION variable in boto3 client calls instead of hardcoded
  "us-west-2"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message: doc: Clarify when disabling HF CAS/Xet is useful

feat: Streaming copy & shared EBS info

Add streaming copy example. Add configuration guidance for storage on
SM NBIs. Move EBS volume and IAM config guidance from Qwen nb(s) up
to import_models parent folder because it should be common challenges
for pretty much all the samples. Reference the ~61GB standard Qwen
30B instead of the specific PoC one. Add gitignore for data files.

doc: Rename for casing and clarity

Capitalize 'Qwen3' since we have caps elsewhere in the name anyway.
Swap 'local'->'Download' because the term local could be confusing
when we're talking about running the notebook on SageMaker (and its
storage requirements mean running on laptop is probably impractical).
cmi-qwen3-notebook

commit.message:
Add Bedrock Guardrails demo for agentic AI with Strands SDK

- Demonstrates ApplyGuardrail API for input/output protection
- Shows tool output protection using agent hooks (AfterToolCallEvent)
- Covers 6 attack scenarios: prompt leakage, task hijacking, task override, tool poisoning, jailbreak
- Includes PII anonymization demo
- Uses Claude Sonnet 4 model
riv demo app
set up code modality guardrail for Kiro
demo of using code modality GR as a Kiro Hook

commit.message: updating readme

Merge #647 Fix Langfuse encryption key generation

Previously there was a small possibility of generating an unsupported
character 'w' in the (hexadecimal) encryption key during Langfuse
deployment.

Merge #647 Fix Langfuse encryption key generation

Previously there was a small possibility of generating an unsupported
character 'w' in the (hexadecimal) encryption key during Langfuse
deployment.

Co-authored-by: Kenny <klampzlamps@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix: Ensure key generation uses only valid hex characters

The encryption key was incorrectly allowing the character 'w' in its output, which is not a valid hexadecimal character.

Merge #605 Langfuse deployment updates & fixes

Extensions and fixes for CloudFormation-based deployment of
self-hosted Langfuse, for the GenAIOps on Langfuse workshop. Added a
mechanism to pass outputs from CDK-deployed stacks back in to the
bootstrap CloudFormation stack - which allows us to surface the
deployed LangfuseUrl to workshop participants more easily without
them needing to go trawl through the CFn console. Swapped out Docker
Hub container image sources by default to GHCR (for Langfuse) and ECR
Public (for ClickHouse) by default, to avoid potential
unauthenticated rate limit issues during deployment. Fixed issue
where SampleDeployment returning new PhysicalResourceId after updates
caused CloudFormation to treat the update as a replacement and
subsequently raise a DELETE request for the old physical ID... Which
ran a cdk destroy and deleted the deployment 🤦‍♂️ Fixed passing of
deployment failure Reason to CloudFormation. Bumped CDK and Postgres
versions.

parent 158df8303483610b580e708ddd897d4cfe3513a2

Merge #605 Langfuse deployment updates & fixes

Extensions and fixes for CloudFormation-based deployment of
self-hosted Langfuse, for the GenAIOps on Langfuse workshop. Added a
mechanism to pass outputs from CDK-deployed stacks back in to the
bootstrap CloudFormation stack - which allows us to surface the
deployed LangfuseUrl to workshop participants more easily without
them needing to go trawl through the CFn console. Swapped out Docker
Hub container image sources by default to GHCR (for Langfuse) and ECR
Public (for ClickHouse) by default, to avoid potential
unauthenticated rate limit issues during deployment. Fixed issue
where SampleDeployment returning new PhysicalResourceId after updates
caused CloudFormation to treat the update as a replacement and
subsequently raise a DELETE request for the old physical ID... Which
ran a cdk destroy and deleted the deployment 🤦‍♂️ Fixed passing of
deployment failure Reason to CloudFormation. Bumped CDK and Postgres
versions.

commit.message: chore(langfuse): bump CDK and Postgres versions
chore(langfuse): update codebuild nodejs runtime

doc(langfuse): Extra CFn bootstrap pattern detail

Link to our dedicated sample discussing the CloudFormation wrapper
pattern, now it's published.

fix(langfuse): Set uid for upstream ClickHouse

Upstream Docker Hub ClickHouse was failing to deploy due to failing
to chown EFS mount from created 1001:0 to root 0:0. Configure
upstream variant to run as non-root user, like Bitnami.

fix(langfuse): clickhouse->ecr for rate limits

Consume ClickHouse containers via Amazon ECR Public (Bitnami
distribution) by default, instead of upstream Docker Hub ClickHouse
(which is still supported) - as some users still facing rate limit
issues.

fix(langfuse): docker->gcr.io for rate limits

Consume Langfuse web and worker containers via gcr.io instead of
Docker Hub, as some users were having rate limit issues

feat(langfuse): Relay outputs to bootstrap CFn

Pass the CDK output LangfuseUrl through as an output from the
bootstrap CloudFormation too, to simplify workshop flow.
Also fixed some other issues found while testing the bootstrap:
1/ Changing PhysicalResourceId reported by CodeBuild was causing CFn
to regard the deployment as a replacement and therefore trigger a
separate `cdk destroy` request afterwards. 2/ some minor issues with
failure reason (logstream) reporting logic.

readme_html:
Amazon Bedrock Samples

To leverage this repository please use our website powered by this GitHub: Website

This repository contains pre-built examples to help customers get started with the Amazon Bedrock service.

Contents

  • Introduction to Bedrock - Learn the basics of the Bedrock service
  • Prompt Engineering - Tips for crafting effective prompts
  • Agents - Ways to implement Generative AI Agents and its components.
  • Custom Model Import - Import custom models into Bedrock
  • Multimodal - Working with multimodal data using Amazon Bedrock
  • Generative AI Use cases - Example use cases for generative AI
  • Retrival Augmented Generation (RAG) - Implementing RAG
  • Responsible AI - Use Bedrock responsibly and ethically
  • Workshop - Example for Amazon Bedrock Workshop
  • POC to Prod - Productionize workloads using Bedrock
  • Embeddings - Learn how to use Embedding Models available on Amazon Bedrock
  • Observability & Evaluation - Learn how Amazon Bedrock helps with improving observability and evalution of Models, Gen AI Applications.

Getting Started

To get started with the code examples, ensure you have access to Amazon Bedrock. Then clone this repo and navigate to one of the folders above. Detailed instructions are provided in each folder's README.

Enable AWS IAM permissions for Bedrock

The AWS identity you assume from your environment (which is the Studio/notebook Execution Role from SageMaker, or could be a role or IAM User for self-managed notebooks or other use-cases), must have sufficient AWS IAM permissions to call the Amazon Bedrock service.

To grant Bedrock access to your identity, you can:

  • Open the AWS IAM Console
  • Find your Role (if using SageMaker or otherwise assuming an IAM Role), or else User
  • Select Add Permissions > Create Inline Policy to attach new inline permissions, open the JSON editor and paste in the below example policy:
{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "BedrockFullAccess",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": ["bedrock:*"],
            "Resource": "*"
        }
    ]
}

⚠️ Note 1: With Amazon SageMaker, your notebook execution role will typically be separate from the user or role that you log in to the AWS Console with. If you'd like to explore the AWS Console for Amazon Bedrock, you'll need to grant permissions to your Console user/role too.

⚠️ Note 2: For top level folder changes, please reach out to the GitHub mainterners.

For more information on the fine-grained action and resource permissions in Bedrock, check out the Bedrock Developer Guide.

Contributing

We welcome community contributions! Please see CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

Security

See CONTRIBUTING for more information.

License
